title: MATRIX Cyber Incident Domain Extension v0.2
description: Domain extension for the MATRIX Core Event Schema v0.2 supporting AI-assisted public-sector cybersecurity incident triage. Activated when the core field domain_extension equals cyber_incident. Designed to preserve cyber resilience, institutional accountability, traceability, auditability, fail-closed governance, and public-sector continuity. Privacy-by-design: no raw personal data, no exploit payloads, no malware artefacts, no offensive cyber content.

incident_id.description: Unique non-personal institutional identifier of the cyber incident, traceable to the issuing SOC or CSIRT case management system. Must not contain personal data.

alert_source.description: Controlled high-level category identifying the originating detection or alerting system that generated the incident signal.

incident_severity.description: Institutional severity classification of the incident based on impact, scope, and continuity risk to public-sector services.

confidence_score.description: Normalized confidence score in the range 0.0 to 1.0 expressing the AI triage assistant's or analyst's confidence that the event constitutes a genuine cyber incident. Advisory only; does not replace human judgment.

threat_vector.description: High-level threat category only. Must not contain exploit details, malware code, payloads, or operational offensive content.

affected_services.description: List of impacted public-sector services expressed exclusively as institutional references or identifiers. Must not contain hostnames, IP addresses, raw technical artefacts, or personal data.

items.description: Opaque institutional reference or registry identifier of an affected service, such as a service catalogue ID or ministry service code.

regulatory_report_status.description: Status of mandatory regulatory reporting obligations, such as NIS2, GDPR breach notification, or sectoral CSIRT obligations. Reflects only the procedural status, not regulatory content.

containment_action_reference.description: Opaque reference identifier to a containment action recorded in the institutional response playbook or case management system. Must not include technical execution details, commands, or offensive instructions. Use PENDING or NOT_APPLICABLE when no containment action has been recorded yet.

communication_status.description: Status of internal and external institutional communication regarding the incident, supporting transparency and public-sector continuity.

SOC_validation.description: Human SOC analyst validation status of the AI-assisted triage outcome. Mandatory human-in-the-loop checkpoint; AI output is advisory only.

CSIRT_notification_status.description: Status of notification to the responsible CSIRT or CERT entity for coordination, escalation, or cross-institutional response.

post_incident_review_reference.description: Opaque reference identifier to the post-incident review or lessons-learned record archived in the institutional governance repository. Use PENDING or NOT_APPLICABLE when no post-incident review has been created yet.

fail_closed_cyber_reason.description: Controlled rationale for triggering a fail-closed response in the cyber domain when AI triage confidence, validation, oversight, or institutional preconditions are insufficient. Ensures that, in case of doubt, the system halts AI-assisted decisioning and defers to human authority.
